title: ajuga japonica
intro: Ajuga japonica, also known as Bugleweed or Ajuga, is a perennial flowering plant native to Japan and parts of Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to inhibit the production of thyroid hormones and its antiviral and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Ajuga japonica include its potential to reduce anxiety and insomnia, as well as its ability to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. Herbal preparations of Ajuga japonica can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to treat a range of health issues, including fever, sore throats, and digestive problems.
